generatedBy: /audit skill v2 · Session 79 hybrid product + infrastructure rubric · game-loop + app-release lenses
summary: Premise-verified Session 79 hybrid audit: repair the Daily Challenge trust break through certified match evidence and durable reward receipts, enforce one atomic project-status mutation path, delete unreachable shipped-looking client code and prevent recurrence, make E2E bootstrap deterministic, converge session parsing, and make the test/coverage contract reflect the production risk surface. Registry and launch observations stay externally blocked and explicit.

sample.method: Main-agent live-code survey plus forked read-only game-loop, release-gate, and infrastructure-debt reviews. Every accepted premise was traced to a live route, caller, store, workflow, parser, coverage artifact, or import graph.
sample.scanned: authoritative progression, Daily Challenge store/API/HUD, player persistence schema, Worker route composition, project-status writers, client import graph, startup/SIL parsers, Vitest coverage, package scripts, E2E/deploy/promote workflows, source map, release evidence, Ark receipts, and live GitHub workflow state
sample.baseline: main synchronized at 86fc03b5; prior closeout reported 139/139 main files and 840/840 tests plus 32/32 server files and 124/124 tests; doctor 8/8 blockingFailing 0. Live release review is NO-GO because the latest E2E workflow failed during native canvas bootstrap and no external staging/identity/email/human/revenue/approval evidence exists.

gameLoop.boundary: The core convoy-pressure loop is coherent, but context/GAME_LOOP.md, a playtest corpus, and three authenticated human Alpha sessions are absent. Daily Challenge is visible but not a functional authoritative progression loop.

passing: immutable digest promotion and rollback documentation
red: latest E2E workflow failed during canvas installation fallback
no external staging origin, parity receipt, or live header/CWV/theme proof
no native Obelisk relying-party credentials or wiring
no three-human Alpha, revenue, or founder-approval receipt

topRisks: Worker.ts is a 4,300-line route god-object with no direct coverage
four scripts bypass the canonical PROJECT_STATUS invariant writer
ten client modules are unreachable from every runtime entrypoint
coverage excludes unloaded production modules and major UI god-objects remain below useful branch floors

evidenceBoundary: Local automation cannot manufacture staging, relying-party identity, project-domain delivery, distinct-human Alpha, revenue, or founder approval.
Registry type is still app while local PROJECT_STATUS and STUDIO_MANIFEST say game. CANON-018 forbids editing the sibling registry; follow-up must travel through Ark.
No new AI surface is justified: the audit found retention and infrastructure trust gaps, not an unmet generative feature need.
No package install, paid service, license change, or sibling tree edit is required for local items. AGPL upstream obligations remain unchanged.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Tune the convoy loop from code review alone
rejectedPremises.evidence: No core-loop timing corpus or three-human Alpha sample exists. Instrumentation and declared success bars must precede tuning.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Wire every orphaned client scaffold into navigation
rejectedPremises.evidence: Several files duplicate mature upstream replay/spectator paths or contain incomplete endpoints and double custom-element registration. Reachability without product need would convert dead code into broken promises.
The gaps include breaking Express, Vite, ESLint, TypeScript, nanoid, uuid, and protobuf majors; a bulk train would obscure compatibility evidence.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Promote local theme proof into launch parity
rejectedPremises.evidence: docs/THEME_LOCAL_PROOF.json explicitly says local-only; it cannot prove an external origin, headers, Core Web Vitals, or deployment digest.

items.title: Rebuild Daily Challenge as a certified, durable Daily Mastery contract
items.why: The HUD fetches a challenge, but normal play never sends an authenticated persistentId to the only progress caller; the store advertises bonus gold no caller applies and clears all progress on restart. This is a visible retention promise that cannot complete honestly.
items.coreLoopStep: certified match actions → exactly-once daily progress → durable mastery reward receipt → next-session return
items.recipe: Restrict challenges to metrics present in the certified match envelope, process batched counts inside MatchProgression, persist per-day progress plus per-game idempotency in Postgres with an honest memory fallback, replace fictitious match gold with durable Mastery points, return typed completion/balance receipts, extract the Daily Mastery HTTP router behind injected auth/store dependencies, update HUD copy, and test duplicate envelopes, rollover, batch completion, persistence SQL, anonymous isolation, and router authorization.

L1.recipe: Remove unsupported challenges and drive progress from certified match counts.

L2.recipe: Add exactly-once in-memory receipts, honest Mastery rewards, HUD refresh, and focused tests.

L3.recipe: Add Postgres persistence/idempotency, extracted authenticated router, typed progression receipt, and transaction-bound tests.

executionLog: Shipped L3: certified metrics, UTC deterministic selection, transactional Postgres event/progress/wallet tables, honest process-local fallback, authenticated injected router, typed progression receipts, HUD refresh/copy, and 7 focused tests.

items.title: Make every PROJECT_STATUS mutation use one atomic invariant writer
items.why: verify-plan-mode, detect-session-mode, render-startup-brief, and project-doctor write PROJECT_STATUS directly despite the declared canonical writer, allowing SIL invariants or concurrent fields to be lost.
items.coreLoopStep: runtime evidence → one validated atomic mutation → trustworthy briefs/doctors → correct founder decision
items.recipe: Upgrade write-project-status to durable temporary-file plus atomic-rename semantics, expose a mutation API, migrate all four direct writers, add a repository-wide bypass checker, wire it into doctor, and test SIL invariant preservation plus unrelated-field retention.

L1.recipe: Replace direct writes with writeProjectStatus calls.

L2.recipe: Use updateProjectStatus and atomic rename with focused tests.

L3.recipe: Add a fail-closed bypass scanner and doctor enforcement.

executionLog: Shipped L3: atomic same-directory temp/rename writer, mutation API migration across four callers, bypass scanner, doctor probe, and 4 focused tests.

items.title: Delete unreachable shipped-looking client code and make orphan features fail CI
items.why: Ten client modules have no runtime importer, mount, or entrypoint; several advertise missing endpoints or duplicate mature replay paths, and some would double-register custom elements if imported.
items.coreLoopStep: feature source → proven runtime reachability → player-visible value, or deletion → smaller honest product
items.recipe: Build a deterministic relative-import graph from the real browser entrypoint, fail on unreachable custom elements/layers in VaultFront-owned source, delete the ten proven orphans, update the source map, and test dynamic-import, extension, index, and intentional type-only edge cases so the gate is not a regex toy.

L1.recipe: Delete the ten proven orphan files and update source documentation.

L2.recipe: Add an import-graph checker and representative fixtures.

L3.recipe: Wire the reachability ratchet into doctor/build and bind it to the public capability truth surface.

executionLog: Shipped L3: production-entry import graph with static/dynamic/re-export/URL edges, 11 proven orphan deletions, corrected source map, CI/doctor contract, and 2 fixture tests.

items.title: Make E2E bootstrap survive canvas prebuild outages and use canonical health
items.why: The latest E2E run failed before tests when canvas prebuild download fell back to node-gyp without pixman. Playwright system dependencies install only after npm ci, and deploy/promote probe /api/health while the canonical release contract names /_health.
items.coreLoopStep: commit → deterministic dependency bootstrap → real browser gate → trustworthy staging promotion
items.recipe: Provision canvas build dependencies before npm ci, pin the workflow Node contract to the repository engine, cache without masking install failures, converge deploy/promote health URLs on /_health, extend the deploy contract checker, and validate workflow semantics locally without pretending the remote rerun already passed.

L1.recipe: Install native canvas prerequisites before npm ci.

L2.recipe: Add Node-engine parity and canonical health route convergence.

L3.recipe: Make workflow/deploy contract tests prove step order and exact health semantics.

executionLog: Shipped L3 locally: Node 22 E2E, native canvas prerequisites before npm ci, canonical /_health convergence, engine contract, and 38-check deploy verifier. Remote rerun remains unclaimed until push.

items.title: Converge startup, freshness, forecast, and closeout on one typed session parser
items.why: Four generated surfaces recognize different Session/S heading grammars; brief freshness even treats prose mentions as authoritative, so a plausible document can silently pick the wrong session.
items.coreLoopStep: append-only session evidence → one parser → coherent startup/forecast/closeout surfaces
items.recipe: Extend session-chronology with typed heading records and strict date/session extraction, migrate sil-forecaster, brief-freshness, startup brief, and closeout board, and add adversarial prose, legacy S headings, out-of-order, duplicate, and malformed-date fixtures.

L1.recipe: Share the same strict heading regex across four consumers.

L2.recipe: Return typed ordered session records with legacy support.

L3.recipe: Add semantic ambiguity rejection and cross-renderer contract fixtures.

executionLog: Shipped L3: typed session sections and strict session evidence shared by freshness, startup, forecast, and closeout with adversarial fixtures.

items.title: Make test and coverage commands describe the real production risk surface
items.why: npm test runs the server suite twice, coverage only includes loaded modules with zero native thresholds, Worker is absent, and the three-file custom ratchet omits the largest client/server risk surfaces.
items.coreLoopStep: code change → non-duplicated representative suite → risk-sensitive coverage receipt → fast trustworthy delivery
items.recipe: Split main and independent server-repeat commands without double discovery, add explicit production coverage.include/exclude rules, extend the ratchet to the extracted Daily Mastery router plus critical progression and major UI seams at evidence-based floors, and test package/workflow contract alignment.

L1.recipe: Remove accidental duplicate server discovery and name the repeat command explicitly.

L2.recipe: Include production modules and add measured critical-module floors.

L3.recipe: Bind local/CI/closeout commands to one machine-checked verification contract.

executionLog: Shipped L3: non-duplicated bounded test scripts, production-inclusive coverage, Worker visibility, 10 evidence-based critical floors, injected Daily Mastery route seam, and CI contract alignment.

items.blockedReason: Studio Ops still reports type=app after prior correction and follow-up cargo; CANON-018 forbids editing the sibling registry or arc-profile producer from VaultFront.
items.title: Receive a source-tagged registry correction receipt
items.why: The arc profiler selected the wrong audit lens again while local source-of-truth consistently says game.
items.coreLoopStep: project truth → source-tagged registry receipt → correct audit/release lens
items.recipe: Ship one evidence-deduplicated Ark follow-up referencing the existing cargos and require Studio Ops to return either a correction receipt or intentional-classification decision. Do not send repeated cargo after receipt exists.

L1.recipe: Surface the mismatch and existing receipt IDs.

L2.recipe: Ship a deduplicated follow-up with current arc-profile output.

L3.recipe: Land and propagate source-tagged reconciliation in Studio Ops after its owner acts.

items.blockedReason: Cloudflare and Brevo capabilities are ready, but no approved external staging origin/environment or Obelisk relying-party registration exists; human Alpha, revenue, and founder approval are non-substitutable observations.
items.title: Exercise the exact digest through a real staging observation corridor
items.why: Local artifacts and contracts are strong, but the release gate is correctly NO-GO without live parity and human/business evidence.
items.coreLoopStep: verified digest → staging observation → human trust evidence → founder-approved launch
items.recipe: After the staging contract exists, deploy the exact digest; bind origin, commit, image digest, workflow URL, headers, CWV, themes, Brevo, and Obelisk into a parity receipt; then collect three authenticated humans, revenue, rollback drill, and founder approval in that order.

L1.recipe: Create approved staging environment and health observation.

L2.recipe: Produce digest parity plus identity/email/theme evidence.

L3.recipe: Complete human Alpha, revenue, rollback, and founder gate with signed receipts.
